title: Write get_grade(score) — return letter grade
task: Write a function get_grade(score) that takes a number (0–100) and returns 'A' for 90+, 'B' for 80+, 'C' for 70+, 'D' for 60+, and 'F' for below 60.
label: conditionals

title: Write get_grade_remark(grade) — return remark
task: Write a function that takes a grade letter ('A','B','C','D','F') and returns a remark: 'Excellent', 'Good', 'Average', 'Below Average', 'Fail'.
label: conditionals

title: Write calculate_average(scores) — return average
task: Write a function that takes a list of numbers and returns their average rounded to 2 decimal places.
label: loops

title: Write get_highest_score(scores) — return max
task: Write a function that takes a list of scores and returns the highest score WITHOUT using the built-in max() function. Use a loop.
label: loops

title: Write get_lowest_score(scores) — return min
task: Write a function that takes a list of scores and returns the lowest score WITHOUT using the built-in min() function. Use a loop.
label: loops

title: Write count_passed(scores) — count passes
task: Write a function that takes a list of scores and counts how many students scored 50 or above. Return the count.
label: loops

title: Write count_failed(scores) — count failures
task: Write a function that takes a list of scores and counts how many students scored below 50. Return the count.
label: loops

title: Write create_student(name, score) — return dict
task: Write a function that takes a student name and score, and returns a dictionary with keys: 'name', 'score', 'grade'. Use get_grade() logic inside.
label: data-structures

title: Write add_student(students, name, score) — add to list
task: Write a function that takes a list of student dicts, a name, and a score. Creates a student dict and appends it to the list. Return the updated list.
label: data-structures

title: Write find_student(students, name) — search by name
task: Write a function that searches a list of student dicts by name. Return the student dict if found, or 'Not found' if not.
label: data-structures

title: Write remove_student(students, name) — delete by name
task: Write a function that takes a list of student dicts and a name. Remove the student with that name and return the updated list. If not found, return list unchanged.
label: data-structures

title: Write list_all_students(students) — print all
task: Write a function that takes a list of student dicts and prints each student's name, score and grade neatly on one line each.
label: loops

title: Write is_valid_score(score) — validate input
task: Write a function that takes a score and returns True if it is a number between 0 and 100, and False otherwise.
label: conditionals

title: Write is_valid_name(name) — validate name
task: Write a function that returns True if a name is a non-empty string with no digits, False otherwise.
label: conditionals

title: Write count_grade(students, grade) — count by grade
task: Write a function that takes a list of student dicts and a grade letter, and counts how many students have that grade.
label: loops

title: Write get_top_student(students) — return best student
task: Write a function that loops through a list of student dicts and returns the one with the highest score.
label: loops

title: Write sort_students_by_score(students) — sort descending
task: Write a function that returns a new list of student dicts sorted by score from highest to lowest. Use bubble sort or any manual sorting — no sorted() built-in.
label: loops

title: Write get_class_summary(students) — return dict summary
task: Write a function that returns a dict with: 'total', 'passed', 'failed', 'average' for the whole class.
label: functions

title: Write update_score(students, name, new_score) — edit score
task: Write a function that finds a student by name and updates their score and grade. Return the updated list.
label: data-structures

title: Write get_students_by_grade(students, grade) — filter
task: Write a function that returns a list of only students who have the given grade.
label: loops

title: Write format_student_report(student) — format string
task: Write a function that takes a student dict and returns a formatted string: 'Name: Amina | Score: 88/100 | Grade: B | Remark: Good'
output: Name: Amina | Score: 88/100 | Grade: B | Remark: Good
label: strings

title: Write get_score_range(students) — return range
task: Write a function that returns the difference between the highest and lowest scores in the class.
label: functions

title: Write has_student(students, name) — check existence
task: Write a function that returns True if a student with the given name exists in the list, False otherwise.
label: conditionals

title: Write get_all_names(students) — extract names list
task: Write a function that takes a list of student dicts and returns a list of just the names.
label: data-structures

title: Write get_trips_by_route(trips, route_name) — filter
task: Write a function that returns all trips for a specific route name.
test:
trips = [{'route_name':'CBD to Westlands','passengers':10,'fare_paid':500},{'route_name':'CBD to Karen','passengers':8,'fare_paid':400}]
print(get_trips_by_route(trips,'CBD to Karen'))

routes = [{'name':'CBD to Westlands','distance_km':5,'base_fare':50},{'name':'CBD to Karen','distance_km':15,'base_fare':100}]
print(remove_route(routes,'CBD to Westlands'))

trips = [{'route_name':'CBD to Westlands','passengers':10,'fare_paid':500},{'route_name':'CBD to Westlands','passengers':12,'fare_paid':600}]
print(get_revenue_by_route(trips,'CBD to Westlands'))

title: Write has_route(routes, name) — check existence

test:
routes = [{'name':'CBD to Westlands','distance_km':5,'base_fare':50}]
print(has_route(routes,'CBD to Westlands'))
print(has_route(routes,'CBD to Mombasa'))
